text: The HATS-GUI shall provide the user the ability to prepare applications.
The HATS-GUI shall facilitate the parsing of transformation language programs and target programs.
The HATS-GUI shall facilitate the execution of transformation language programs.
The HATS-GUI shall facilitate the examination of SDT and pretty-printed output from transformations.
The HATS-GUI shall facilitate the creation, deletion, and modification of the files related to an application.
The HATS-GUI shall manage file associations within an application.
The HATS-GUI shall capture data written to debugging and error streams and make that data available to the user.
The HATS-GUI shall associate saved files with the application.
The HATS-GUI shall allow the user to select files for display.
The HATS-GUI shall provide an interface for the user to search for text strings and sub-trees.
The system shall be platform independent and function properly on any operating system.
The system shall be tested under Sun Solaris,Windows 2000,and Linux.
The system shall be designed to minimize the number of windows opened by the application.
The system shall be developed using the Java programming language.
The system shall be completed by December 2001.
Any operation requiring the user to supply a file name shall allow the user either to select the files from a list or to key in a file name.
The user shall be able to cancel any operation where the user is prompted to select from a list.
The HATS-GUI shall overwrite any file whose name is generated automatically when collecting output files.
The HATS-GUI shall prompt the user for a file name for the file to be saved.
The HATS-GUI shall prompt the user to overwrite the existing file if a file with the given name already exists.
Output pretty-printed text files and SDTs shall only be displayed when the user requests.
The main interface shall be menu driven.
The HATS-GUI shall allow the user to specify the number of levels of nodes to expand during SDT viewing.
The HATS-GUI shall allow the user to select the colors and shapes for types of displayed nodes.
The HATS-GUI shall provide an interface for the user to enter implementation-dependent information for starting HATS-SML processes.
The HATS-GUI shall notify the user if it fails to start a HATS-SML program.
The HATS-GUI shall be able to communicate with Sun Solaris,Linux,and Windows 2000.
The HATS-GUI shall interact with the HOST OS file services to create,delete,open,read,write,and close files.
All files associated with a given application shall be stored in one directory named with the application name.
The HATS-GUI shall suggest a file name for the user whenever it prompts the user to enter a file name.
The HATS-GUI shall display output written to the standard output and standard error streams in a tab-selectable window.
The system shall open a new window when the user selects to display output.
Each active display window shall have a mouse cursor and a keyboard cursor.
The keyboard cursor shall be movable by the arrow keys.
The HATS-GUI shall allow the user to import a file into the selected application by copying it into the application directory.
The HATS-GUI shall allow the user to continue working while transformations are taking place.
The HATS-GUI shall allow the user to execute a script file containing a linear sequence of HATS-SML programs.
The user shall be able to select an arbitrary set of displayed nodes in a displayed graph.
The HATS-GUI shall provide for the navigation of the SDT using a navigation window and scroll bars.
The HATS-GUI shall provide for the copying of text from the text display area to a system buffer.
The HATS-GUI shall display SDTs as directed acyclic graphs with nodes and edges.
The HATS-GUI shall provide the function to fully expand a sub-tree.
The HATS-GUI shall display an SDT with 10,000 nodes in five seconds or less.
The complexity of the text and tree search algorithms shall not exceed O(n^2) for n nodes.
The user shall not be required to log in or authenticate his/her identity.
The system shall be implemented in the Java 1.3 programming language.
